title: Orchestrator Prompt — Foundation
status_field: DRAFT — pending human doc-ratify (authored 2026-07-06, Orvex Studio adaptation of Houston's skCyy79mCF)
what_it_is: The Foundation Orchestrator operating prompt, run PER REPO across the Studio family. It defines eight strictly-ordered, per-repo milestones (M1 Zero-Mock Tidy through M8 No-Op OpenAPI Skeleton) that take a single repo from 'mock-driven prototype' to a build-ready, enterprise-grade, test-driven foundation (CI on the self-hosted `runner` group, build-only k8s manifests with ESO wiring, dual-IdP hardening, OpenAPI-contracted 501-sentinel skeleton). It explicitly identifies itself as the companion/substrate doctrine that the Delivery Orchestrator (gkkUDzn277) cites and consumes at its §1, and is invoked live via the `/orchestrator-studio-foundation` skill which always re-fetches this page (never a stale mirror). Several concrete values are marked 'TBD — set during the Studio Act-1 run' (mock-quarantine location, OpenBao secret paths, orchestrator execution seat, family/repo sequencing).
overlaps: No direct content conflict with the Delivery Program (5eFdxN3edd) or its phase orchestrators (P1 yXUWpQpRjx, P2 Ng66su4dVG, P2.5 ErYdXzIj6g) — they operate on a different axis (per-repo build-readiness stages M1-M8 vs. program-level phases 0-3) and this page is the machinery those phase prompts reuse, per the task brief. However: (1) this page's reference list cites gkkUDzn277 (Delivery Orchestrator), the family canon root, coding standards, URL scheme, Delivery Prompts landing (KNZMCOKAV8), Issue Authoring Prompt, and SE-Arch Review Agent, but contains NO link to the Delivery Program plan-of-record (5eFdxN3edd) or to the P1/P2/P2.5 phase orchestrator prompts — a reader landing here cold has no breadcrumb forward to the current 4-phase model, and could mistake the M1-M8 numbering for a rival/parallel sequencing scheme to Phase 0-3. (2) Its open TBD placeholders (mock-quarantine location, OpenBao secret paths, execution seat, cross-repo family sequencing) are exactly the kind of concrete decisions the newly-authored parallel-build-plan.md (8-teams-by-service, contract-tag unlock) needs to pin down when it lands in the wiki (folded into the Phase-2 Isolated Builds prompt or as a child of 5eFdxN3edd) — if that new plan resolves 'family sequencing' independently without updating/cross-linking this page's TBD list, the two documents will silently diverge on the same open question. Recommend the new parallel-build-plan page, once landed, add a forward link here (or this page add one to it) at the 'family sequencing: TBD' note.
action_detail: Keep this page as-is (it is live, cited, invoked machinery — do not fold or archive). Add a short breadcrumb near the top (next to the existing 'Canon refs' line) pointing to the Delivery Program 5eFdxN3edd as the current plan-of-record and naming which phase(s) invoke this Foundation Orchestrator (per-repo, likely within Phase 0 Stabilize / per-repo inside Phase 2 Isolated Builds), so the M1-M8 per-repo stage numbering is never confused with the Phase 0-3 program-level numbering. When the new parallel-build-plan lands (folded into the P2 Isolated Builds prompt or as a 5eFdxN3edd child), have it resolve/cross-link this page's 'TBD — set during the Studio Act-1 run' items (mock-quarantine location, OpenBao paths, execution seat, family sequencing) rather than re-deciding them independently.
